Name: Laura Mark
About and First met notes:
Laura Mark is an award-winning architecture critic, curator and filmmaker based in London.
She is the Keeper of Walmer Yard and runs the Baylight Foundation, where she curates a number of cultural programmes and projects which look at how we experience architecture.
She teaches at the Bartlett School of Architecture and the University of Greenwich and was previously a Visiting Lecturer in the BArch programme at the Birmingham School of Architecture and Design. She also runs a unit at the annual summer programme – Studio in the Woods. At the beginning of lockdown, Laura launched a series of events titled the Virtual Unit, designed to support students who were now completing their studies at home. Laura has been a guest critic at numerous schools of architecture including Sweden’s Umea University, Sheffield School of Architecture, Reading School of Architecture, Bartlett School of Architecture, Central St Martins, and the Royal College of Art.
Trained as an architect, Laura spent five years in architectural practice before joining the editorial team of the Architects’ Journal. She went on to become the magazine’s Digital Editor, where she oversaw a comprehensive redesign of its digital offering. Later she became the magazine’s Architecture Editor, looking after every aspect of its coverage. During her time at the magazine, Laura won numerous awards for her work including IBP’s Multi-Media Journalist of the Year in both 2015 and 2016. Her writing has featured in magazines including the Architectural Review, Domus, Dezeen, RIBA Journal and Icon.
She has directed a number of films including the documentary Zaha: An Architecture Legacy (2017) which has been shown at festivals in London, Milan, New York and Miami. Laura is currently working on a new series of films entitled Practice, which follow the studio culture of architects from Piers Taylor and Sam Jacob to Mary Duggan and O’Donnell and Tuomey. The first episode, which was released in February 2020, has been shown at the Dezeen Virtual Design Festival, Palm Springs Architecture, Design, and Art Film Festival and New York Architecture Design Film Festival.
In summer 2020, Laura was named by the London Festival of Architecture and the Design Museum as one of ‘an emerging generation of voices in architecture who are expanding the parameters of what architecture can be, who London is for and what its future holds’.
She is currently undertaking a PhD which surveys the cultural history of the senses in architecture at the University of Newcastle.

Rob joined Bray Fox Smith in 2019 after 10 years Montagu Evans. Rob is a senior, experienced Central London office agent with a proven track record and strong client following. He has advised private investors, developers, landed estates, institutions and tenants in all aspects of commercial office space and mixed-use property. This includes major office leasing assignments; long-term development consultancy projects; development site sales and acquisition; and corporate occupier representation focusing on acquisition, disposals, lease restructuring, consolidation, cost cutting and turn-key solutions. His goal is to grow the scale and capability of the Central London agency offer at BFS, working alongside the other market leading decentralised experts already in the firm. 

Rob’s property career spans 30 years, with his Agency life starting at Strutt & Parker, before moving on to Weatherall Green & Smith, Jones Lang LaSalle and penultimately Montagu Evans, where he helped build and diversify the agency only teams into efficient multi discipline sector groups. Rob was a Regional Director at JLL before joining Montagu Evans as a full profit sharing partner in 2009.
Born in Stockholm, Sweden, but has since spent most of his formative years in England, living in Surrey, where he went to Kingston Grammar School, and then studied Estate Management in Southampton. Rob lives in Notting Hill and has a daughter and a son who are at Bristol University and Winchester College respectively.

Rob regularly used to play hockey for Surrey county, athletics for the Borough of Kingston, row for University, and ski since he could walk. Since his sporting heyday, he has since competed in several triathlons and long distance swimming events, and now enjoys restoring classic cars, classic boats and country cottages. He also has a habit of collecting interesting vintage “stuff”.

Name: Patrick O’Gorman
About and First met notes: Got his details to make an intro from James Morgan (CHP) on an email on 28/04/21
📞Recent Updates:
Really cool asset manager/ developer. Got the details to make an intro to them from James Morgan (CHP) on an email on 28/04/21.
Theo Michel and Patrick  O'Gorman came to our office on 11/05/21 to meet DL and JJ. 
Theo and Richard Walker (owner of Iceland) started it after they met as graduates at JLL. Patrick joined later and also invests in the projects (met when he was at CBRE Poland).
Patrick is a good communicator and seems to be the people's person. Theo seemed quite serious at the start and more relaxed later on.
Not an easy start as they had been told by James Morgan that we had something more specific. They are all about sustainability and said that they like being transparent about what they know and don't know. Their investors and tenants are all the time asking them difficult questions about sustainability. They understand that it is a long road and they are here for the long run.
They hate green-washing.
They are looking for commercial/ office spaces in central London. From £50M-£200M and are keen to see how buildings can be refreshed. 
They are working with Feilden Clegg Bradley but sometimes they get a partner to pitch and then they deal with an associate they don't like. They like dealing with them in Belfast because they are a team of 12 and a great associate runs it.
They are seeing that at their Paradise building Feilden Clegg Bradley and Buro Happold are overdoing certain stages and feel that they don't seem to understand that often is best to deal with certain things after planning to keep costs down.

He has been in the office (Farringdon area) for 2 weeks now and was very happy. He was the youngest partner ever at Quantem until got beaten by somebody else who was a month younger. He said he had a business breakfast (with BW workplace) lined up amongst other social events and quite excited about that. He does commercial offices and industrial sheds mainly. However at CHP they do a bit of everything. Working loads with The Office Group (their offices are on a TOG building). He will see if he can make an intro. CHP are similar size to us with 30 years of experience. Set up by 3 founding partners, 2 still work there. They have a NYC and a London office. Historically in the US the QS side of things has been done by the PMs and this is changing now. Also a few developers work on both cities. He brought a few projects (including 102-108 Clerkenwell Road) and clients from Quantem. Knows Rob Rotbart (Astrea) well, caght up a year ago but told Rob that he was moving and therefore to wait to give him a job so he is catching up with him in 2 weeks. He mentioned they are pitching for an investment vehicle created by New Forest (he said it was called Quails(?) - couldn't find it) where there is no framework and there is only one QS and one PM. He will see if he can make an intro. They are happy to do high level work for us.
UPDATE - JJ breakfast 27/07/21
Said that Henry (Endurance Land) likes early breakfasts as early as 7.30am. Said that Bywater are struggling to get insurance for the CLT structure and might have to go for a hybrid of steel beams with rest on CLT. Said that V7 are very busy.
They are working with RE Capital, Dorrington and Opera PMs (get in touch with them)
He left Quantem because he felt stuck. He knows Adrian Lim well and also that he is leaving to Quartz. He is now a proper partner and if he hits his revenue goals over the next 3 years he will become an equal partner of CHP (20%). They are an LLP so it is difficult for a partner leaving to cash in. For Limited companies he says that a way to sell the business is to build an employee owned trust that buys the company and pays the shareholders. That means that the value is less that in the market but makes it easier to sell. However you need to be profitable for the following 3 years.
Had a coffee with Rob Rotbart 2 weeks ago but he didn't have anything for him at the time. They have a NYC office just to service London clients that want to do things there. The partners here get all the profits of the NYC office and the partner there only gets a massive salary.
The said he could see if he can intro us to the office group.

He says there is a moment were it is difficult to progress your career as a freelancer so decided to be a full time employee as MD (too around 7 months and 6-7 interviews). Has 2 twin kids at uni studying real estate at different unis. They have found it difficult to study remotely and he has been paying for their empty student accommodation for a while now. Their 5 year plan is to grow by about 60-70%. Dicky mentioned Robin BrodieCooper at Gleeds who he knows too, Mark said that he is the only nice person at Gleeds. Name: Nicada Drayton
About and First met notes: Adrian Lim made an intro on 24/05/21
👤Intro to: Oscar Lawrence (https://www.notion.so/Oscar-Lawrence-704de241dd2b4e6688b3d3e53ec2cf55?pvs=21), Will Atkinson (https://www.notion.so/Will-Atkinson-7cad12944561454db56cd3d08ec60360?pvs=21), Adrian Lim (https://www.notion.so/Adrian-Lim-ef3a478795744bd087670fa9d25a937b?pvs=21)
📞Recent Updates:
JH/JJ call with her on 08/06/21
We got an intro from Adrian Lim (Quantem), they have been working with them for some time and seem to be looking for more options for an architect.
She said the senior management changed recently and implied they are not 100% happy with what they are getting from the architects they have always used (she didn't say because she was being careful but was clear).
We showed our selected work portfolio and Southampton Optivo and she really liked it. They finished recently a job in Southampton and don't seem to be 100% happy with it. She said it would have been best to retain more of the existing, look at VEing options, etc. She noted that they had to comply with HMO requirements for this job (as seems to be the case for all their jobs) and noted that Optivo probably don't need to do this because they are a Housing Association (we should have known that).
She wanted to see more projects to show that we get the space requirements for student resi (dig out Folkestone).
She is working on a job in Richmond that is at feasibility stage. In the summer they will refurbish 2 properties. They have a 10 year old portfolio and they are about to refurbish a lot of it. Just sold 4 buildings so have capital to invest. They will start by refurbishing 40 rooms in 2 cities as a test and, if profitable, will roll this out to the rest of their portfolio.
She said they are a young company like us and that they give a bespoke service. Buildings are max. 50-200 rooms.
They want to have more city presence. Their preference is to have 200 rooms across 3 building instead of a large building with 200 rooms (as an example). They target international market, and understand they appeal to these students (city centre location, 10-20 min to university).
Often they do quite a traditional FF&E.
Preference is more studios than clusters.
They have a new head now (she then said he is actually not that new) who will 'try to make sense of their portfolio over the next 2 years'.

UPDATE
Call on 07/01/2022 to set up coffee.
He lives in Liverpool, wife from Belfast (they were there in Christmas). They used to do work with L&G but thinks that the Director moved on. Majority of their worked used to do cut and carve in central London.
Comes a couple of days a week to London.

UPDATE - zoom call on 20/01/22
Rolfe Jubb architecture and planning departments are almost like different contracts. He thinks that when they use their own architecture teams corners are cut. They went from 120 to 90 through recession. They are prob now 65-70 in arch and 25 for planning. The arch team don’t like smaller jobs which he thinks it is a mistake.
Neal’s Yard was a bit awkward for him because his relationship has always been with Fresson and Tee (PM) and suddenly they are dealing with Paragon. He says that F&T had made some mistakes and Shaftesbury might be fed up.
Personally 30-40% of what he does is Shaftesbury.
They do loads in the city.
He also works with The Max Barney Estate which he says would be a good fit for us. Jewish family, they are quite relaxed in their approach and own big chunks of Shoreditch. The main guy is a former builder and takes pride in knowing what can and cannot get built. They use Kyson Architects a lot (he works with them and really likes their style). He will try to put us forward next time there is an opportunity.
A colleague of his works quite a bit with The Berners London Estate.
They do a lot with Workspace Group

UPDATE Coffee 21/02/21
Charlie Owen is going to The Crown Estate (he was today with Rupert). No replacement for now, sounds like Phil Canby will take over from him.
Says that F&T are the ones dealing with Chinatown and Seven Dials portfolio. They’ve made some mistakes recently but reckons Shaftesbury will still use them.
The Max Barney Estate don’t use internal PM/QS. A guy called Scott Payne (Linkedin messaged him on 21/02/22) has had some issues with Scott Kyson. The issues seem to come with jobs that should be straightforward and Kyson overcomplicated things. Therefore he is looking at using other architects but being careful given their relationship.
His colleague Clare is working with The Berners-Allsop Estate and another small estate in Kensington (he didn’t remember the name of this one) so he will have a word with her. He is in London for a couple of nights and the following day he was going to be at a hearing he knows they are going to lose. He said that he hates how architects (especially at Rolfe Judd) are negative and also often quite idealistic in the wrong way (they almost try to impose their ideals saying ‘this is who I am’)

UPDATE - He is leaving Rolfe Judd to join a Planning Consultancy firm that is more involved with schools

About and First met notes:

They are working with them on a Cat A refurb that is on site at the moment (finishing Jan/ Feb 2022) at Grenville Street, Farringdon. 30,000sqft office, being done by Groupwork Architects (they are doing a couple of projects with them). Young guy (our age). He likes a drink. Peter had had drinks before the day we had the coffee and one of his guys at Quantem stayed with Chris until 1am. Doesn't have kids, lives in Balham.
UPDATE - DL/ JJ & Peter Morgan breakfast on 11/02/22
Married, started on Seaforth 2 years ago after being on a 3 month garden leave. Likes snorkelling, born in Zambia (?) and his dad lives in south Africa. Going to the Mauritius on holidays soon.
Space House is keeping them busy and they have a couple of large jobs also in the pipeline but says that they want smaller ones as the large ones take a large time to push forward. He loves mountain riding and is starting to learn kite surfing. 
Tyler the boss is Canadian and the money comes from Canada.
He is actively looking at quite a few things and wants to share something with us when the right opportunity comes. Conscious that he doesn’t want to waste our time unless there is a good opportunity

UPDATE - Breakfast on 28/10/22
Had a great time, good catch up. He is going up and going to start filling up Marcus’ shoes. Got engaged in June and they are getting married next year in Marbella. They will prob do the extension in a couple of years time. Seaforth sounds exciting as Tyler is a proper visionary and both him and the other main guy don’t seem to be worried about the recession. Quite the opposite they seem to be excited by what opportunities this period could bring. Chris things that the current projects are great but he worries about the pipeline. Loves exposed CLT and what it does to the acoustics. Teo and somebody else are the ones who look at many deals and anything that is remotely interesting then comes to him. Ares are very risk adverse, that’s why 77 didn’t happen. He is not doing much BD but seems keen to do more. He can intro me to a really good friend of him who is a PM on Birmingham and recently set up his own thing. He is doing very well and looking at some things in Manchester and working with clients such as Stanhope. Keen to get that intro to James Harrison

Name: Martin Fantl
About and First met notes:
Richard Nelson and Martin Fantl came to our office and met with DL and JJ on 28/04/21. JJ had already met Richard on a call. Martin is a really nice guys, very relaxed, has been at WT since he left university decades ago. Said that it is impressive that we have doubled our size in 2020. Martin was born in the Czech Republic and came to the UK as a kid. Speaks both Czech and German and at the start of his career worked in Portugal (Algarve) and Barcelona (Hotel project). Martin was impressed by our shortlisting on the RIBA competition, they have 900 people in China (Hong Kong) and wants us to do a CPD with their team on the competition and sustainability. His father knew China well as he used to travel there, there was a good connection between both countries back in the days because they were communist countries. He said that his father recommended him to go to many places that have now disappeared as many heritage areas have been destroyed. They agreed with DL that Buckley Gray Yeoman are dropping the ball on some projects. Their Louis Vuitton project
They are working with Aviva and said that they would try to make an intro. They tend to use the same architects so it is a bit of a difficult one but that they will try. They do have a small 2,000-3,000sqft job where everybody is getting frustrated with the design. The easier course of action is for the client to keep the same designer but they are keen to change their mind so will see if there is an opportunity there. Martin is more PM side and they have a team of 40 PMs in London, he wants to come to Thursday drinks. Richard has an apartment in Almeria
📞Recent Updates: Dropped him an email on 29/04/21 re the small potential job, the CPD and Thursday drinks

Name: Mark Syrett
About and First met notes: Connected through Linkedin.
Position: Managing Director (London, South East & Channel Islands)
Status: 8. Not worth following up in future
📞Recent Updates:
JJ zoom intro call on 29/04/21. It was both Mark Syrett (started 8 months ago) and David Hobbs (started 26 years ago). They pitch for multidisciplinary bids like one recently for Crown Commercial Services (they are waiting to hear back from it) and they said we could do it with them. They have different MDs for different regions. They have 650 people in the UK and are 2,000 globally. They are part of a 20,000 staff group. They are massively expanding their Zero Carbon arm. They do a lot in China and have some presence in India. David is QS Head, a team of 35 in London. They are working on the Red Sea development. They have done a lot of heritage including jobs like the palace of Westminster. They are interested in helping with some high level figures for Burr Street, Mark said that it would be a good opportunity to get our teams to know each other. I said that sounds good but after planning in a month as don't want to waste their time. Up for Thursday drinks and they will bring Max from their team who is PM side.
UPDATE: JJ & DL breakfast with Mark Syrett and David Hobbs on 06/07/21. David is head of the QS team and fairly quiet, didn't look as comfortable with BD. Mark is very relaxed and seems to enjoy himself, prolonged the breakfast a bit and was not in a rush. First time in town for Mark since October 2020, he said he was a bit uneasy due to covid and had unfortunately kind of got used to working from how. However understands that his job is to be out and about and they will try to get the office at 50% capacity from July 19. He has worked at many places and for 19 years he freelanced at high level positions. He says there is a moment were it is difficult to progress your career as a freelancer so decided to be a full time employee as MD (too around 7 months and 6-7 interviews). Has 2 twin kids at uni studying real estate at different unis. They have found it difficult to study remotely and he has been paying for their empty student accommodation for a while now. Lives near Guildford. He is a chartered QS and so is his wife. They met at uni and she was the only female in their year. They worked at Gleeds together which he says it is a shit company and badly run. He was there both early in his career and later on and nothing had changed. When he announced at the pub that he was getting engaged with his now wife everything changed and started treating them badly. Says women were harassed/ molested/ upskirted on a regular basis and it was acceptable.
He will introduce us to others that are younger in their team. There is a guy called Max from PM side that he kept on saying that we should meet. Max started 6 months ago and is always out and about and bringing loads of smaller projects. He said we should also meet Alan at their sustainability team. They now have a sustainability team of 12 who are really pushing that side of the business. In the UK they do a lot more public than they do private and want to balance things by doing more private. Their 5 year plan is to grow by about 60-70%. Dicky mentioned Robin BrodieCooper at Gleeds who he knows too, Mark said that he is the only nice person at Gleeds. They have an informal internal team of people that are pretty much the 'next gen guys'. Mark offered to grab another coffee in the future to have another chat if we need help. Should consider him as an informal mentor.

UPDATE: coffee at their office on 01/02/2022
Met with Mark, David and Max. They are really busy. He said he had recently had dinner with Martin Thomas at Hanover Green (the have been mates for 20+ years) and that he was very complimentary
Mark was pushing to see if we can work on something together

Name: Richard Estrop
📞Recent Updates:

Emily has been with them for 6 year and is now a Director. She just bought a house (4 bed) out of London 10 houses down from her parents. No kids, getting married in July 2021 and getting stressed about the lifting of the restrictions by 21st June. They are relying on this to move forward with the wedding as they don’t want a reduced wedding with no dancing. Wedding will be in the UK. She was in Tomatina in Spain before covid. Richard is a big F1 fan, he broke his leg (femur) 2 years ago and he is still using a crutch. Marcus was there when he slipped and fell. Now drives his bmw everywhere. Puts a Jamaican flag scent thing in the central mirror when he is in a dodgy neighbor as anti-theft system. Founding partner.
Marcus mentioned that Shaftesbury is one of their clients. He lives far from London near the sea and a great golf course. He golfs and says it is great with clients. He says they didn’t plan to be 150 when they started. Stressful last 12 months both personally and at work. At work they had to reduce the team but he says it was a good opportunity to get rid of people they didn’t want. They reduced their salary by 20% and the staff salaries by 5-10%. They gave all the money back and now are recruiting. At personal level he was trying to buy another house and to get it off the market signed a contract to rent it for a year for £100k but only managed to complete last minute against all odds. Said the stress was extreme and slept for two days after that.
Works internationally and has done quite a bit in Europe. His mum lived in Hong Kong for 7 years (she is back in the UK now) and he has high net worth HK clients. Also good Middle East clients including from Qatar who buy trophy assets in Central London. Dicky mentioned an off-the-market deal that he was interested in. DL to share it.
Emily and Marcus know Bill Soper, Sam Watkins, etc. Marcus said that Bill is letting people down and implied he should retire.
